title: 10 best herbal teas for indigestion

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian teas helps with indigestion because of its unique ability to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve digestion. The tea's bitter properties help to increase gastric juices, which in turn aids in breaking down food properly. Additionally, yellow gentian has anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, providing relief from symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yellow gentian tea contains flavonoids and other anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and discomfort.

Digestive enzymes stimulation: The bitter compounds in yellow gentian tea stimulate the production of digestive enzymes in the stomach and intestines, which helps to break down food more efficiently and relieve symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and gas.

Spasmolytic properties: Yellow gentian tea has been shown to have spasmolytic properties, meaning it can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce spasms that can contribute to indigestion and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yellow gentian plant contain alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, making them effective in relieving indigestion.
Rhyzomes: Yellow gentian rhyzomes are used to prepare medicinal teas due to their ability to stimulate digestion, relieve bloating, and reduce flatulence.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried gentiana lutea root or four teaspoons of fresh root.
Boil one cup of water in a tea kettle for five minutes and then remove from heat.
Add the harvested gentiana lutea root to the boiling water and steep for eight to ten minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or mug.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consumption and drink slowly to alleviate indigestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica teas helps with indigestion because of its natural soothing properties. The herb contains volatile oils, such as angelic acid and ligustilide, which help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping. This gentle relaxation also aids in calming inflammation and discomfort associated with indigestion. Additionally, angelica's carminative properties help to ease bloating and gas, promoting a smoother digestion process. As a result, drinking herbal angelica tea can provide effective relief from indigestion symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Angelica tea helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to indigestion and discomfort.
Carminative: The tea's carminative properties help to alleviate gas and bloating by preventing the formation of gas bubbles and promoting their release from the body.
Anti-inflammatory: Angelica tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to indigestion, nausea, and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and fiber, angelica roots have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues and ease symptoms of indigestion.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the angelica plant contains a high concentration of volatile oils, which helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried angelica archangelica root and leaves in the morning for optimal potency and flavor.
Measure one teaspoon of dried herb or two teaspoons of fresh herb per cup of boiling water.
Steep the herb mixture in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the bioactive compounds and essential oils.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids after use.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it immediately, as angelica's carminative properties are most effective when consumed warm or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with indigestion because its soothing properties gently calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The herb's mucilages coat the stomach lining, protecting it from acid reflux and irritation, while also easing spasms and cramps. As a natural antacid, marshmallow tea neutralizes excess stomach acid, alleviating symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and heartburn, promoting a sense of relief and comfort for those struggling with digestive discomfort.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and coats the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with indigestion.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion like bloating and cramps.

Digestive enzymes inhibitors: Marshmallow tea has been shown to inhibit certain digestive enzymes that can contribute to indigestion, allowing for better digestion and reducing symptoms of discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm digestive issues, making it an effective treatment for indigestion.
Barks: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in the stomach, providing relief from indigestion symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marshmallow roots althaea officinalis from your garden or local health store measuring about 20-25 grams.
Rinse the harvested roots under cold running water removing any dirt and debris gently patting dry with a paper towel.
Chop the rinsed marshmallow roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or a food processor processing until coarsely chopped for 5 minutes.
Combine the chopped marshmallow roots with 1 cup of boiling filtered water in a medium-sized pot steeping for 10-15 minutes to release flavors and nutrients.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large teapot discarding solids and serving hot as needed to soothe indigestion symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway teas helps with indigestion because it contains a unique combination of volatile oils, specifically carvone and limonene, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with indigestion. Additionally, caraway's antispasmodic effects relax the muscles in the digestive system, easing cramps and spasms that can contribute to indigestion symptoms. By promoting healthy digestion and reducing inflammation, herbal caraway teas provide natural relief from indigestion and related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Carvone content: Caraway tea contains a high concentration of carvone, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help soothe digestive tract muscles and reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines.

Antioxidant activity: Caraway tea is rich in antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, common factors contributing to indigestion.

Choleretic properties: Caraway tea has been shown to stimulate bile production and release, which helps improve digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating, cramping, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Caraway roots have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues and calm stomach upset, making them an excellent choice for indigestion relief.
Seeds: Caraway seeds contain a compound called cuminaldehyde that has natural antispasmodic properties, helping to ease cramps and discomfort associated with indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Measure out two teaspoons of dried caraway seeds for every cup of boiling water.
Steep the caraway seeds in the boiling water for five minutes to allow the flavors and nutrients to infuse.
Strain the tea leaves from the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Discard the solids and pour the tea into a cup to serve immediately.
Drink the tea within 30 minutes of preparation while still warm to aid in digestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with indigestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation caused by spicy or fatty foods. The apigenin in chamomile also binds to benzodiazepine receptors in the gut, calming muscle contractions and promoting relaxation, which can ease symptoms of bloating, cramps, and discomfort. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system help regulate digestion and reduce stress-related stomach upset, making it a gentle and effective natural remedy for indigestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe digestive issues such as indigestion, by reducing inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining.
Antispasmodic effects: Chamomile's antispasmodic properties can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps, spasms, and discomfort associated with indigestion.

Gastroprotective compounds: Chamomile contains gastroprotective compounds that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux, gastritis, and other digestive issues, thus alleviating symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the gut, making them effective for indigestion relief.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing spasms and cramps that can occur during indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes to allow the herbs to infuse and release their properties.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids.
Add honey to taste if desired, but avoid mixing in other ingredients that might interfere with digestion.
Drink the warm tea immediately after preparation and before eating or as needed throughout the day to alleviate symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal teas helps with indigestion because it contains berberine, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. When consumed as a tea, goldenseal can help soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and calming muscle spasms in the stomach and intestines. Papaverine: Exhibits relaxant properties that can help alleviate muscle spasms in the digestive tract, relieving cramps and discomfort associated with indigestion.

Barks: The barks are rich in alkaloids, which have natural antispasmodic effects that can help ease cramps and spasms associated with indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried goldenseal root powder or four cups of fresh leaves.
Combine the measured goldenseal with one cup of boiling water in a medium-sized pot.
Reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes allowing the mixture to steep.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Drink the warm tea immediately after straining and enjoy the digestive benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with indigestion because of its natural properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fennel leaves or use dried fennel flowers and seeds for a reliable source of digestive relief.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried origanum majorana leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for one cup of tea.
Measure out one teaspoon of the herb for every eight ounces of boiling water and place it in a heat-resistant teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the herb and allow it to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any loose particles or debris.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving, enjoying warm or at room temperature as needed to alleviate indigestion symptoms.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat indigestion with herbal teas is to opt for calming and soothing options like peppermint, chamomile, and licorice root. Peppermint tea helps relax the digestive system, reducing symptoms of bloating and discomfort. Chamomile tea calms the stomach and reduces inflammation, while licorice root tea soothes heartburn and acidity. Drink 1-2 cups per day to alleviate indigestion symptoms naturally.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to indigestion that are treated with herbal teas are heartburn, bloating, and acid reflux. Ginger tea, in particular, is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the stomach and alleviate these symptoms. Other herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and licorice root also have calming effects on the digestive system, making them effective natural remedies for these discomforts.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for indigestion are a natural and soothing remedy for individuals suffering from digestive issues. These teas are made from plants such as peppermint, chamomile, and licorice root, which help to calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation. By promoting healthy digestion, herbal teas can alleviate symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and discomfort. For example, peppermint tea has been shown to improve symptoms in individuals with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), while ginger tea can aid in reducing nausea and discomfort.
